extraction.one_line_summary: CATALDO Dario won from a 200km+ breakaway with CATTANEO Mattia, holding off NIBALI Vincenzo and CARAPAZ Richard who gained time after ROGLIČ Primož crashed/mechanicaled in the finale.
tactical_patterns: MITCHELTON SCOTT forced the pace for 40-50km before finale, setting up YATES Simon's attacks but fragmenting protection for others.
NIBALI Vincenzo exploited final climb descent attacking when ROGLIČ mechanical/crash created chaos, gaining crucial 40+ seconds on GC rivals.
YATES Simon attacked multiple times on final climb without waiting for coordination, collecting bonus seconds but not distancing CARAPAZ Richard.
ROGLIČ Primož lost 40s not from climbing weakness but mechanical/crash chaos amplified by BAHRAIN high pace stranding him behind.

rider_signals.CATALDO Dario: positive: survived 200km+ breakaway with 15min maximum gap, still had sprint power to win after aggressive pace-setting by peloton
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: lost 40s to GC rivals through mechanical/crash bad luck during finale when BAHRAIN and others attacking, first time loss
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: recognized opportunity when ROGLIČ crashed, attacked on final climb he knows from Lombardia, descended aggressively to gain time
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: multiple attacks on final climb only yielded bonus seconds, moved to 8th GC but couldn't distance CARAPAZ
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: marked all YATES attacks, followed NIBALI move, retained pink despite MITCHELTON SCOTT forcing all day
rider_signals.CATTANEO Mattia: positive: stayed in 200km break with over 15min gap, led out sprint positioning despite ultimate second place
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: attempted move on final climb but couldn't sustain gap against BAHRAIN counterattacks
